tokenization_rule: lowercase split-on-whitespace-and-punctuation; remove stopwords; compare token-sets

per_cluster_check.sub_mechanism_match_against: max overlap M_i vs C1.claimed_coverage = 1 token (routing)
M_1 vs claimed_coverage (structured-graph expert adjacency): shares {graph,adjacency} ≥ 2
M_3 vs claimed_coverage (expert specialization via grouping): shares {experts,group}
M_2 vs claimed_coverage (efficiency seed + stripped surfaces prior art): shares {routing,adjacency}

gap_position_rationale: Candidate sits inside 6/6 hypothesized clusters. The Petersen-graph specific structure is a particular case of the broader 'structured-graph expert-adjacency' cluster family (C2, C5, C6 directly cover; C1 covers fixed routing; C3 covers structured sparsity; C4 covers clustering-based grouping). The hypothesized landscape anticipates the candidate at the conceptual level even though no specific Petersen-graph MoE paper is in the retrieved set.
